preamble: The transformation from a voiced aspirated bilabial plosive ([bʰ]) to a voiceless labiodental fricative ([f]) is a well-documented historical sound change, notably occurring in the development of Latin from Proto-Indo-European. This shift represents a significant lenition process, where a complex stop consonant simplifies and becomes more open, while also losing its voicing and aspiration.
phoneticEffects: Deaspiration, Devoicing, Fricativization (Lenition), Place of Articulation Shift (Bilabial to Labiodental)

examples.note: Proto-Indo-European root meaning 'to carry' to Latin 'I carry'.
Proto-Indo-European root meaning 'to be, to grow' to Latin 'I was'.

sources: Source via Google Search grounding (verify before merge)
